transcript_facts:
- [Assessment] well , let's start with your diabetes .
- [Assessment] so , you know , my impression of you at this time , you know , from a diabetes standpoint , let's , let's increase the metformin to 1,000 mg twice a day .
- [Findings] yeah , i've been kind of managing through my depression , and , uh , my asthma's been acting up 'cause we had a really bad pollen season , and i am at least keeping my diabetes under control , but just , uh , it's just persistent issues all around .
- [Findings] are you having a lot of side effects from that ?
- [Findings] okay , well , let's , let's go ahead and we'll do a quick physical exam .
- [Findings] hey , dragon , show me the pfts . okay , so your , your pfts , that , those are your breathing studies , and those look quite good , so i know that you're wheezing right now , but , um , you know , i think that we can add , add , um , a regimen to that to help , to help you with your , um , exacerbations during the , the summer months , okay ?
- [Findings] show me the hemoglobin a1c . okay , so your a1c , you're right , you know , over the past couple months is , you know , your blood sugar's probably been running a little high , so , you know , i know that you're gon na get back on your diet regimen , but , um , for right now , let's go ahead and we'll increase your metformin , okay ?
- [Findings] show me the chest x-ray . okay , good , and your chest x-ray looks fine , so we know that there's no pneumonia there .
- [Findings] um , and , we will get a repeat hemoglobin a1c in three months , and i want you to continue to monitor your blood sugars at home .
- [History of Present Illness] have you had any dizziness , lightheadedness , fever , chills ? running up and down the stairs , it was pretty warm , so i did feel a little bit lightheaded , and i did get a little dizzy , but i thought it was just the heat and the fatigue . any chest pain , shortness of breath , or belly pain ? shortness of breath . no belly pain though .
- [History of Present Illness] and , how about any joint pain or muscle aches ?
- [History of Present Illness] um , and , in terms of your asthma , you just said that you were short of breath running up and down the stairs , so , um , do , how often have you been using your inhaler over the past year ?
- [History of Present Illness] if it's a nice , cool , dry day , i really do n't use the inhaler .
- [History of Present Illness] and then , your depression , you said it's ... how's that going ? i know we have you on the , on the prozac 20mg a day .
- [History of Present Illness] i thought i felt a little bit better , but i think , uh , i , i kinda go through battles with depression every so often .
- [History of Present Illness] are you interested in resuming the medication , or would you like to try a different one ?
- [History of Present Illness] so , looking at you , you're in , in no apparent distress . there's no cervical lymphadenopathy . your thyroid seems not enlarged . and , listening to your lungs , you do have some bilateral expiratory wheezing that's very faint , and your heart is a regular rate and rhythm . your abdomen is soft , and uh , your lower extremities have no edema .
- [History of Present Illness] and , it's just is all just from your asthma .
- [History of Present Illness] from an asthma standpoint , let's continue you on the albuterol , two puffs , uh , every four to six hours as needed , and we'll add symbicort , two puffs twice a day during the summer , to kind of help prevent those exacerbations .
- [Plan] joseph is a 59 year old male here for routine follow-up of his chronic problems .
- [Plan] and then , from a depression standpoint , we'll go ahead and start you on a different medication , zoloft , um , 25 mg once a day and see how you tolerate that .
